description_long: This obelisk is a vertically oriented, monolithic structure with a roughly rectangular cross-section and a slightly tapering form towards the top. The surface appears to be carved from a light-colored stone, with visible weathering and irregularities that suggest age and exposure to the elements. The front face features a prominent, high-relief sculptural motif, possibly depicting a robed human figure or symbolic form, while the sides are less detailed but show some textural variation. The base is broad and flat, providing stability, and is set on a square plinth that appears to be part of the same stone material. The tip of the obelisk is blunt and irregular, lacking the sharp pyramidal point typical of some obelisks, and shows signs of erosion or damage. There are no visible inscriptions or text, but the sculptural relief adds a decorative and historical character to the monument.
description: A weathered stone obelisk with a sculpted relief on its front, set on a square plinth and featuring a blunt, irregular tip.
description_view_0: The rear view shows the plain, unadorned back of the obelisk with visible shadows and a flat surface.
description_view_1: The left side view reveals the depth of the relief carving and the thickness of the obelisk, with the plinth visible at the base.
description_view_2: The front view highlights the sculpted relief and the broad, stable base, with the blunt tip clearly visible.
description_view_3: The right side view shows the profile of the relief and the overall tapering form of the obelisk.
attribute_ornamentation: A high-relief sculptural motif, possibly a robed human figure, is present on the front face of the obelisk.
attribute_base_design: The base is a broad, flat, square plinth made of the same stone as the obelisk, with no visible decorative features or steps.
attribute_tip_design: The tip is blunt and irregular, lacking a sharp point and showing signs of erosion or damage.
